front: Dynasty: founded in 960 CE by Zhao Kuangyin (Taizu), who overthrew the Later Zhou to end the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period
work: General / Giveaway Identifiers

front: Dynasty: divided into a Northern period (capital Kaifeng) and a Southern period (capital Hangzhou), ending in 1279 when the Mongols established the Yuan dynasty
work: General / Giveaway Identifiers

front: Dynasty: renowned as culturally significant but militarily weak, credited with inventing gunpowder, movable type, and the magnetic compass
work: General / Giveaway Identifiers

front: Ruler: this dynasty's founder claimed his soldiers forced him to declare himself emperor after waking him from a drunken snooze
work: Founding: Taizu and the End of the Five Dynasties

front: Ruler: this founder hosted a banquet at which he persuaded all his generals to resign their military commands
work: Founding: Taizu and the End of the Five Dynasties

front: Event: this Song founder's death, in an incident known as 'the shadow of the candle and the sound of the ax,' and the dynastic transition it caused is the subject of the Naito Hypothesis
work: Founding: Taizu and the End of the Five Dynasties

front: Ruler: this Song founder interrupted the scholar Fan Zhi's dinner in tears to announce his coup
work: Founding: Taizu and the End of the Five Dynasties

front: Ruler: this man was the Song dynasty's second emperor, succeeding its founder Taizu
work: Founding: Taizu and the End of the Five Dynasties

front: Institution: the Song dynasty defended its northern frontier with this fortification, nicknamed for its excavated form
work: Northern Frontier Wars: The Liao, the Jin, and Yue Fei

front: Event: a Song emperor signed this treaty, elevating the Khitan state of Liao to equal status with the dynasty
work: Northern Frontier Wars: The Liao, the Jin, and Yue Fei

front: Event: the Jurchen-led Jin dynasty besieged and captured the Song capital Kaifeng, taking Emperors Qinzong and Huizong prisoner in this 1127 incident, which ended the dynasty's Northern period
work: Northern Frontier Wars: The Liao, the Jin, and Yue Fei

front: Place: refugees fleeing the fallen Song capital Kaifeng swelled the population of this city, which became the new Southern Song capital
work: Northern Frontier Wars: The Liao, the Jin, and Yue Fei

front: Figure: this Song general, tattooed with characters meaning 'serve the country with utmost loyalty,' helped defend the Southern Song against Jurchen invaders until he was betrayed and executed
work: Northern Frontier Wars: The Liao, the Jin, and Yue Fei

front: Figure: this Song chancellor betrayed the general Yue Fei, leading to his execution
work: Northern Frontier Wars: The Liao, the Jin, and Yue Fei

front: Work: a Song ruler wrote the preface to this military manual, which detailed the innovation of volley fire with crossbows
work: Northern Frontier Wars: The Liao, the Jin, and Yue Fei

front: Figure: this Song chancellor's 'New Policies' included the Green Sprouts Act, giving farmers credit for seeds to boost agricultural production
work: Wang Anshi's New Policies and the Reform Controversy

front: Work: this document, submitted to the Song throne in 1058, promoted meritocracy and the use of specialists in government
work: Wang Anshi's New Policies and the Reform Controversy

front: Institution: this Song local-defense system, part of Wang Anshi's New Policies, organized households into groups of tens and hundreds
work: Wang Anshi's New Policies and the Reform Controversy

front: Figure: this conservative Song statesman opposed Wang Anshi's New Policies and authored the Zizhi Tongjian (Comprehensive Mirror to Aid in Government)
work: Wang Anshi's New Policies and the Reform Controversy

front: Event: opponents of the Song New Policies were convicted in this trial for 'denouncing the imperial chariot'
work: Wang Anshi's New Policies and the Reform Controversy

front: Event: the Su brothers formed this faction to oppose bureaucratic reforms that built on the earlier Qingli Reforms
work: Wang Anshi's New Policies and the Reform Controversy

front: Event: this earlier Song reform push, championed by Ouyang Xiu and Fan Zhongyan, developed the baojia system and revived the Classical Prose movement
work: Wang Anshi's New Policies and the Reform Controversy

front: Battle: the Song dynasty lost control of the Yangtze River after the Mongols used new trebuchet technology to win this six-year siege
work: The Mongol Conquest and the Fall of the Song

front: Battle: at this naval battle, the last stand of the Southern Song against Kublai Khan's forces, the victors played festive music while hidden soldiers chained enemy ships together
work: The Mongol Conquest and the Fall of the Song

front: Place: a Mongol army seeking to attack the Song from the west instead invaded the Tran dynasty and conquered this kingdom, rather than crossing the Yangtze
work: The Mongol Conquest and the Fall of the Song

front: Battle: Song forces won a notable victory over the Mongols at this engagement, also known as the Battle of Diaoyu Fortress
work: The Mongol Conquest and the Fall of the Song

front: Work: this Song official's Dream Pool Essays first described the iron-needle magnetic compass
work: Science and Technology

front: Work: this book, written during the Song dynasty, gave the first chemical recipe for gunpowder and described its use in fire lances
work: Science and Technology

front: Work: this Song book described fossilized plants exposed on a riverbank, later called the first description of climate change
work: Science and Technology

front: Work: this Song encyclopedic book explained that the sun and moon are balls, correctly identified a 'marine snake' as fossilized, and described a night-sky 'pearl' later interpreted by some as a UFO
work: Science and Technology

front: Figure: this Song scientist built a hydraulic (water-powered) clock tower using the first endless chain drive, for the dynasty's first capital
work: Science and Technology

front: Figure: this Song artisan invented movable type
work: Science and Technology

front: Work: shortages of copper coinage led the Song dynasty to create these, the first paper banknotes in world history
work: Science and Technology

front: Work: this Song judge's book Collected Cases of Injustice Rectified, considered the first work of forensic science, recorded solving a murder identified by a sickle wound
work: Science and Technology

front: Work: this Song mathematician's Mathematical Treatise in Nine Sections featured methods for solving polynomials of up to the 14th degree
work: Science and Technology

front: Figure: this Song female poet wrote a treatise on the ci lyric form and fled Kaifeng after it was sacked by the Jurchens
work: Literature and Poetry

front: Figure: this Song poet-statesman wrote of wishing to 'ride the clouds back home' and helped canonize the earlier poet Tao Qian
work: Literature and Poetry

front: Work: this Shi Nai'an novel, set during the Song dynasty, fictionalizes an early-12th-century revolt and grants amnesty to 108 bandit 'Stars of Destiny'
work: Literature and Poetry

front: Work: this Song 'daytrip essay' is a premier example of the form
work: Literature and Poetry

front: Figure: this Song scholar compiled two of the dynasty's 'Four Great Books,' Prime Tortoise of the Record Bureau and Finest Blossoms in the Garden of Literature
work: Literature and Poetry

front: Work: this compilation of Chinese surnames in a rhyming quatrain scheme was composed during the Song dynasty
work: Literature and Poetry

front: Concept: Song thinkers Cheng Yi and Zhu Xi developed this philosophical movement by mixing Buddhist ideals into Confucian thought
work: Literature and Poetry

front: Figure: this Song emperor depicted himself playing the guqin zither in his painting Listening to the Qin, and pioneered a calligraphy style called 'slender-gold'
work: Painting, Ceramics, and the Visual Arts

front: Painting: this only extant copy of Zhang Xuan's painting, depicting court women preparing fabric, was created by a Song painter
back: Court Ladies Preparing Newly Woven Silk
work: Painting, Ceramics, and the Visual Arts

front: Work: a Song emperor's art collection was recorded in this 20-chapter catalogue of paintings with biographies of over 230 artists
work: Painting, Ceramics, and the Visual Arts

front: Figure: this Northern Song painter developed a form of multiple perspective known as the 'angle of totality' and used characteristic 'crab claw' trees in works like Early Spring
work: Painting, Ceramics, and the Visual Arts

front: Painting: this Zhang Zeduan scroll depicts the Song capital Kaifeng, including a camel caravan passing through a stone gate and a crowd yelling at a boat about to crash into the Rainbow Bridge
back: Along the River During the Qingming Festival
work: Painting, Ceramics, and the Visual Arts

front: Concept: this Chinese landscape painting style, whose name translates to 'mountain-water,' depicted tiny houses and bridges nestled among vivid blue-and-green mountains during the Song dynasty
work: Painting, Ceramics, and the Visual Arts

front: Work: this landscape theme, depicting modern-day Hunan Province, rose to popularity in the Song dynasty and was catalogued in Shen Kuo's Dream Pool Essays
work: Painting, Ceramics, and the Visual Arts

front: Work: the Song court used this crackled-glaze pottery, while the Longquan kilns began producing celadon pottery during the dynasty
work: Painting, Ceramics, and the Visual Arts

front: Work: this style of Chinese porcelain, which English potters later tried to compete with, developed during the Song dynasty
work: Painting, Ceramics, and the Visual Arts
